description: Ticket #4891 — user says their static site at /home/user/www isn't accessible. They've got index.html and some assets in there, just need a basic http server on port 8080. Python's already installed, don't need anything fancy, just get it serving so they can hit localhost:8080 and see the page.
truth:
Initial state:
    - /home/user/www exists and contains index.html plus a css/ subdirectory with style.css.
    - index.html is a valid HTML file with a <link> to css/style.css and visible text content "Welcome to my site".
    - Python 3.x installed, http.server module available.
    - No server currently running on port 8080.
    - /home/user/www is readable by the agent user.
    - curl installed.

    Expected final state:
    - A process is listening on port 8080.
    - `curl -s http://localhost:8080/` returns content containing "Welcome to my site".
    - `curl -s http://localhost:8080/css/style.css` returns 200 (non-empty CSS content).
    - The server process remains running after the agent's session ends (backgrounded or daemonized).

    Invariants:
    - /home/user/www/index.html and /home/user/www/css/style.css byte-identical to initial state.
    - No additional files created in /home/user/www.

    Anti-shortcut guards:
    - The server must actually be serving from /home/user/www — verified by curl returning the real file contents, not a stub response.
name: task_000000_97a36505
